messages.author: TKW1/Seagull ZL1JFM
messages.text: Passed through Brizzie last night. Said hello and received a repeater advert. Is it intended that the radio config for Brizzie will also serve right up the coast?
messages.content_html: Passed through Brizzie last night. Said hello and received a repeater advert. Is it intended that the radio config for Brizzie will also serve right up the coast?

messages.author: James Cridland
messages.text: Hello, @andymac4182 - there are some folk online here. Not too many but growing every day. Meshcore AUS Frequency: 923.125Mhz SF:8 BW:62.5 CR:5
messages.content_html: Hello, @andymac4182 - there are some folk online here. Not too many but growing every day. Meshcore AUS Frequency: 923.125Mhz SF:8 BW:62.5 CR:5
messages.reactions_html: 3

messages.author: 🦦 Hilton Travis SMBI
messages.text: @Fish has a few repeaters around. I am working with him to get the Southern Moreton Bay Islands connected across and also down to Gold Coast. I'm also working on contacts on NSI to get some repeaters on Minjerribah to connect different population areas there and the mainland.
messages.content_html: @Fish has a few repeaters around. I am working with him to get the Southern Moreton Bay Islands connected across and also down to Gold Coast. I'm also working on contacts on NSI to get some repeaters on Minjerribah to connect different population areas there and the mainland.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: James Cridland
messages.text: Just put “4066 Repeater” live in Brisbane. I think this coverage map is wildly optimistic but it should get into West End and the CBD pretty well.

messages.content_html: I have seen 4065 Repeater, but not 4066 yet
messages.reply_html:
James Cridland
Just put “4066 Repeater” live in Brisbane. I think this coverage map is wildly optimistic but it should get into West End and the CBD pretty well.

messages.author: James Cridland
messages.text: 4065 is mine too. I don't think 4065 can see Wallace directly. My suspicion is that when 4066 bursts into a little more life, we might certainly see it in West End and the CBD, so it should hit the CBD-based repeaters. I can't see 4066 from 4065, I don't believe. It should have done some adverts by now. 4066 is just a Heltec v3. 4065 is a more beefy Heltec v4 with a decent antenna.
messages.content_html: 4065 is mine too. I don't think 4065 can see Wallace directly. messages.author: James Cridland
messages.text: Here’s a little update on the bits of Brisbane I can see on the mesh. 23 nodes on the map, 33 contacts currently seen. (4066 Repeater, mentioned above, appears to be sending adverts but those don’t always appear to be visible for some reason). Settings, links etc: https://wiki.mbug.com.au/en/Meshcore/Settings (edited)

messages.text: Can't pick up any from where I live, the map says there's one in Cairns (at least there was a month ago) but haven't had checked yet as only just started experimenting with MC. There are about 17 MT nodes in Cairns though, stretching up to Kuranda.
messages.content_html: Can't pick up any from where I live, the map says there's one in Cairns (at least there was a month ago) but haven't had checked yet as only just started experimenting with MC. There are about 17 MT nodes in Cairns though, stretching up to Kuranda.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: 🦦 Hilton Travis SMBI
messages.text: When I find the time, I'm going to build up a MC repeater backbone across SMBI and with a few decent links over to the mainland for redundancy. Only the very occasional MT contact seen here, and that's when walking along the western coast of Macleay Island.
messages.content_html: When I find the time, I'm going to build up a MC repeater backbone across SMBI and with a few decent links over to the mainland for redundancy. Only the very occasional MT contact seen here, and that's when walking along the western coast of Macleay Island.

messages.text: MC definitely seems like better option for Brisbane area, but up here with like 17 nodes in 100km MT seems fine
messages.content_html: MC definitely seems like better option for Brisbane area, but up here with like 17 nodes in 100km MT seems fine

messages.author: 🦦 Hilton Travis SMBI
messages.text: Ab. So. Lutely. Especially good with meshes over a long distance with many hops. MT can squeeze out 7 hops whereas MC can do up to 64, making wider area meshes as easy as smaller meshes or longer hop meshes.
messages.content_html: Ab. So. Lutely. Especially good with meshes over a long distance with many hops. MT can squeeze out 7 hops whereas MC can do up to 64, making wider area meshes as easy as smaller meshes or longer hop meshes.
messages.reply_html:
🐌🔥
Does MC offer improved connection over long hops?

messages.text: Didn't realise the limit was 64 with MC, that's insane! Time to do some testing
messages.content_html: Didn't realise the limit was 64 with MC, that's insane! Time to do some testing
messages.reactions_html: 1
